item.title: How Poland’s fate drove the Declaration
item.description: Poland was carved up in 1772. Four years later, Americans took note.

item.title: To beat socialists and populists, liberalism must get radical
item.description: It became the ideology of the establishment it once existed to attack. That’s why it’s losing.

item.title: What if Trump is right to pump the brakes on the most advanced AI?
item.description: His policy deserves careful appraisal, not the reflexive dismissal it’s gotten.

item.title: Divorce is getting hairier. Just ask the family pet.
item.description: State legislatures are dealing with the changing legal status of dogs and cats.

item.title: What's behind the South's sudden boom
item.description: Millions of Americans have moved to the South in recent years. The usual explanations — lower taxes, cheaper housing and warmer weather — are only part of the story. Host Megan McArdle explores the economic and historical forces driving the century-long transformation of America’s fastest-growing region.

item.title: Who is winning the AI battle?
item.description: Whoever controls artificial intelligence will win the 21st century. In this episode of “Make It Make Sense,” James Hohmann talks with Kyle Chan, a foreign policy fellow at the Brookings Institution, about the AI race between the United States and China, how Beijing is deploying the technology and whether fears of a Chinese AGI takeover are just hype.

item.title: A top nuclear expert on what the Iran war accomplished
item.description: David Albright says the claim that Iran emerged stronger from the war is simply bunk.

item.title: This law keeps guns out of the hands of drug addicts, if it’s enforced
item.description: The FBI’s background check database should include more drug addicts.

item.title: Alexi Lalas drives U.S. soccer fans crazy. He also helped create them.
item.description: He’s loud, smug and uninformed. He’s also a symbol of how far American soccer has come since 1994.

item.title: How a deadlocked Congress led to Washington
item.description: The capital could have ended up in Pennsylvania. Hamilton and Madison brokered compromise.

item.title: We’re dermatologists. Sunscreen isn’t the problem.
item.description: Every year, a safe-sunscreen guide sparks needless concern.

item.title: Noxious language infiltrated Trump’s Iran deal
item.description: Why is Washington pledging to “refrain from interfering” in Tehran’s internal affairs?

item.title: A punching bag. ‘A graveyard.’ A new temptation for Trump.
item.description: Reaching a better future for Lebanon means getting Israel and Iran out.

item.title: Trump botches an easy win on housing
item.description: A bipartisan reform bill would help marginally boost construction of new homes.
